notes: Ported from the original Cashiro FaysalBankParser.kt (parser-core/src/main/kotlin/com/ritesh/parser/core/bank/FaysalBankParser.kt). Faysal Bank (Pakistan) app notifications + SMS: outgoing IBFT ("sent to"), incoming RAAST/IBFT/FT ("received ... from"), debit card purchases, and ATM cash withdrawals. Known deviations from the Kotlin parser (see fixture comments): - Kotlin normalizes the Pakistani dot-as-thousands amount format ("PKR 55.000.00" -> 55000.00) by collapsing all but the last dot. The engine only strips commas and has no dot-collapsing transform, and a multi-dot amount string is not a valid decimal, so those bodies deliberately extract NO amount and surface as REVIEW/MISSING_AMOUNT rather than a wrong-magnitude value. Comma-formatted and plain amounts are normalized faithfully. - Kotlin requires "pkr" AND a transfer keyword in isTransactionMessage; the filter can only express the keyword OR-list. A non-PKR body with a keyword would fall through to MISSING_AMOUNT/REVIEW instead of REJECTED.

fixtures.name: outgoing IBFT with dot-thousands amount
fixtures.body: PKR 55.000.00 sent to DEMO RECIPIENT A/C *9901 via IBFT from FBL A/C *1234 on 06-FEB-2026 02:22 PM Ref # 960855.

fixtures.body: ACCOUNT HOLDER A/c # *1234 received PKR 500.00 via RAAST from SENDER ALIAS IBAN *3867 on 06-Feb-26 at 04:22 PM Ref#:121621592909 Info:111060606

fixtures.name: incoming IBFT with comma amount
